title: Delicate Flaky Venezuelan Pork Empanadas
description: A traditional Venezuelan dish featuring pork shoulder, these empanadas are perfect for a sick day, with a bitter and flaky crust, and can be shaped like nigiri for a unique touch, making them shojin-friendly
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large pan, heat 2 tablespoons of oil over medium heat and cook the pork shoulder until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 3 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Stir in the cumin, paprika, salt, and black pepper, and cook for 1 minute.
In a large bowl, combine the flour and cold butter, and use a pastry blender or fingers to work the butter into the flour until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
Add the beaten egg and water, and mix until the dough comes together in a ball.
Turn the dough out onto a floured surface and knead for 5 minutes, until smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Roll out each ball into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
Place 1 tablespoon of the pork mixture in the center of each circle, and fold the dough in half to form a half moon shape, pressing the edges to seal.
